title: cynoglossum coeruleum var. johnstonii
intro: Cynoglossum coeruleum var. johnstonii, also known as the Johnston's Hound's Tongue, is a plant species within the borage family, characterized by its blue flowers and unique tongue-like shape. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits associated with Johnston's Hound's Tongue include reducing swelling and fighting off infections, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its uses and effectiveness may vary depending on the specific preparation and individual needs.
